title: Buttery Aerated Brazilian Dinner Dumplings
description: A unique Brazilian dish consisting of light and airy dumplings served in a lacquerware bowl, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the strong white flour, lard, and salt.
Add the eggs, warm water, and mix until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball of dough into a disk shape.
Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add 2 disks of dough to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes on each side.
Add 1 sweet plantain to each cooked dumpling and cook for an additional minute.
Transfer the dumplings to a lacquerware bowl and drizzle with remaining butter.
Chop the fresh parsley and sprinkle it on top of the dumplings.
